summary:
Claim: Smallpox is the rarest condition among those listed because it has been completely eradicated.  
Evidence: - Smallpox was declared eradicated in 1980 and has no natural cases reported since. - Other conditions like cherubism or dental cysts, although uncommon, still occur in children and are recognized in clinical practice.  
Answer: D

(Word count: ~470)

The claim states that smallpox is the rarest condition among the options due to its global eradication. Two pieces of evidence support this: first, smallpox has been officially eradicated since 1980 with no naturally occurring cases reported afterward; second, other disorders such as cherubism or dental cysts, despite their infrequency, remain clinically observable and documented in pediatric patients. The correct answer is therefore D — smallpox — because it exhibits absolute rarity via absence rather than mere low prevalence. This distinction makes it fundamentally rarer than any other option on the list.
